topics: 🐂🕳️ Kal va-chomer between שור and בור — why Torah writes both
📜 Each av needs its own pasuk because each carries unique halakhot
🛏️ Parallel example: משכב and מושב in the laws of zav
🔥 Parallel: parshat neirot vs. parshat shiluach tmeyim
🤕 R' Yose: one who injures another still pays even after he himself becomes injured

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Why Each Av Gets Its Own Pasuk
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: R' Bun bar Chiyya, in the name of R' Shmuel bar Rav Yitzchak, runs a kal va-chomer between שור and בור and shows that either could have been derived from the other. The Yerushalmi answers: דְּאִית לֵיהּ מִילִּין סַגִּין — the Torah states each one explicitly because each category carries its own unique halakhot that a derivation could not generate. The sugya then brings two parallel cases — משכב/מושב and the parshiyot of נירות / שילוח טמאין — to show that this hermeneutic move is a general principle.
overview.hebrewSource: אִילּוּ לֹא נֶאֱמַר שׁוֹר הָיִיתִי לָמֵד שׁוֹר מִן הַבּוֹר... וְלָמָּה תְנִינָתָהּ הָכָא. דְּאִית לֵיהּ מִילִּין סַגִּין

slides.title: 🔁 R' Bun bar Chiyya's Kal Va-chomer
slides.subtitle: Try to derive the bull from the pit (and the pit from the bull).

nodes.content:
If 'שור' had not been written
I would learn it from בור

מָה אִם הַבּוֹר שֶׁאֵין דַּרְכּוֹ לֵילֵךְ וּלְהַזִּיק חַיָּיב לְשַׁלֵּם
שׁוֹר שֶׁדַּרְכּוֹ לֵילֵךְ וּלְהַזִּיק לֹא כָּל שֶׁכֵּן

columns.label: Direction A
columns.content:
Bor pays full damage
→ so should shor

columns.label: Direction B (פרכא)
columns.content:
Shor (tam) pays half damage
→ maybe bor should also pay only half?

nodes.content: Same kal va-chomer runs in both directions — neither category is fully provable from the other

callout.label: Why this matters:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi is not really trying to eliminate one of the avot. It uses the kal va-chomer to ask the deeper question: why does the Torah waste words by writing both?

slides.title: 💬 So Why Does the Torah Write Both?
slides.subtitle: The Yerushalmi's quick, sharp answer.

lines.speaker: Q
lines.text: If shor and bor can each be derived from the other, וְלָמָּה תְנִינָתָהּ הָכָא? — why does the Torah write both?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: דְּאִית לֵיהּ מִילִּין סַגִּין — because each av carries many unique halakhot a derivation could never generate.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: This is a hermeneutical principle: a kal va-chomer is overruled when each side has independent details the other side lacks.

callout.label: Yerushalmi style:
callout.text: Not every kal va-chomer needs to win. Sometimes the Torah writes both terms precisely because each one needs its own halakhic life.

slides.title: 🪞 Parallel Cases of the Same Move
slides.subtitle: The Yerushalmi shows this is a general principle, not just nezikin.

boxes.title: 🐂 שור / 🕳️ בור
items: Each could be derived from the other
Bor pays full damage, shor (tam) pays half
Each has unique laws → both must be written

boxes.title: 🛏️ משכב / 🪑 מושב
items: Mishkav of a zav: 4 tefachim makes it tamei
Moshav of a zav: 1 tefach makes it tamei
Each could be learned from the other, yet both written

boxes.title: 🕯️ נירות / שילוח טמאין
items: Each section adds halakhot the other lacks

callout.label: The shared pattern:
callout.text: When two parallel pesukim could each be derived from the other, the Torah keeps both because each carries מילין סגין — its own unique laws. This is exactly the third middah of R' Yishmael: a כלל that is taught from one parsha.

slides.title: 📐 Mishkav vs. Moshav — A Closer Look
slides.subtitle: The Yerushalmi's tightest illustration of the rule.
rows: Smaller surface area still becomes tamei
Bigger surface needed because of intended use
Carry tum'at zav — but each via its own pasuk

callout.label: Why both pesukim:
callout.text: Knowing only mishkav, you might think nothing smaller than 4 tefachim becomes tamei. Knowing only moshav, you might think any tefach-sized item suffices. The Torah needs both to fix the shiur exactly.

slides.title: 🤕 R' Yose's Ruling on Sequential Damage
slides.subtitle: A halakhic landing point at the end of the daf.

steps.content: 2️⃣ Later, that same person becomes injured himself.
3️⃣ Question: does the later injury reduce or cancel his liability?
4️⃣ Source: וּמַכֶּה בְהֵמָה יְשַׁלְּמֶנָּה — read as יְשַׁלֵּם פְּחָתָהּ.
5️⃣ Ruling: he still pays for the original injury, even after he himself was hurt.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: Liability is fixed at the moment of damage. A later change to the מזיק's own status does not unwind what he already owes.

summary.principle: Each av needs its own pasuk because each carries unique halakhot a kal va-chomer cannot generate. The Yerushalmi proves this with three parallel cases.

flowSteps.content:
R' Bun bar Chiyya's challenge
אִם לֹא נֶאֱמַר שׁוֹר הָיִיתִי לָמֵד שׁוֹר מִן הַבּוֹר
Kal va-chomer runs in both directions → no clean derivation

🎓 Key Principle:
When two parallel pesukim each carry independent halakhot, the Torah writes both. Liability is then fixed at the moment of damage — as R' Yose teaches at the close of the daf.

quiz.q: What is R' Bun bar Chiyya's opening kal va-chomer about?
o: Why שור and בור can each be derived from the other
Whether שן and רגל share a category
Whether אדם is included in the four avot
Why fire damage is not in the Mishnah
quiz.e: He says: if 'shor' had not been written, I'd learn shor from bor — and vice versa. The kal va-chomer can run in both directions.

quiz.q: What is the Yerushalmi's answer to why the Torah writes both שור and בור?
o: Because they are different animals
דְּאִית לֵיהּ מִילִּין סַגִּין — each carries unique halakhot a derivation can't generate
Because the kal va-chomer is invalid
Because of a gezerah shavah
quiz.e: Even when a kal va-chomer works, the Torah states each parsha independently because each one has unique halakhot that need their own source.

quiz.q: Which parallel case does the Yerushalmi bring to illustrate the same hermeneutic move?
o: Tum'at met vs. tum'at sheretz
Mishkav and moshav of a zav
Hekdesh and ma'aser sheni
Tzitzit and tefillin
quiz.e: The Yerushalmi explicitly compares שור/בור to משכב/מושב — each could be derived from the other, yet both are written.

quiz.q: What is the difference in shiur between mishkav and moshav?
o: Both require 4 tefachim
Mishkav 1 tefach, moshav 4 tefachim
Mishkav 4 tefachim, moshav 1 tefach
There is no shiur for either
quiz.e: The Yerushalmi states: moshav becomes tamei in 1 tefach, mishkav requires 4 tefachim. Each pasuk is needed to fix its own shiur.

quiz.q: What does R' Yose teach at the end of the daf?
o: If you damage someone and are later damaged yourself, you still pay
A pit owner is exempt if he warned passersby
Fire damage is always full damage
Half damage applies only to keren
quiz.e: אָדָם שֶׁחָבַל בַּחֲבֵירוֹ תְּחִילָּה אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁחָזַר וְנַעֲשָׂה נִזָּק חַיָיב. Liability is fixed at the moment of damage.
